name: Andaman and Nicobar Islands
stopoverNote: Fly into Sri Vijaya Puram/Port Blair (IXZ); there is no rail link and sea passenger ships are slow, infrequent and not practical for short holidays. Island-hopping depends on fixed ferry sailings by government ferries and private catamarans such as Makruzz, Nautika and Green Ocean, so book Port Blair–Swaraj Dweep–Shaheed Dweep sectors ahead and treat every ferry as a half-day transfer. Tourist travel is limited to permitted Andaman areas; Nicobar Islands, tribal reserves and any contact with protected tribes are restricted and must not be attempted.

diet.vegetarian: Seafood dominates local menus, but vegetarian travellers are comfortable in Port Blair, resort-heavy Swaraj Dweep and Shaheed Dweep: ask for veg thali, dal-rice, dosa/idli, poha, veg biryani, paneer/veg curries and tropical fruit. Baratang and jetty-side stops are basic, so carry snacks before early ferry or road departures.
diet.vegan: Vegan food is possible but needs clear requests because island kitchens often use ghee, butter, paneer, curd or mayonnaise, and remote day tours have fixed meals. In Port Blair and larger Havelock resorts, ask for oil-only dal, rice, roti without ghee, vegetable curry, coconut-based chutneys and fruit; on ferries and Baratang, carry backup snacks and confirm dairy-free cooking a day ahead.
phrases: Shakahari khana chahiye, machhli ya meat nahi = I need vegetarian food, no fish or meat
Doodh, dahi, paneer, makkhan aur ghee mat daliye = please do not add milk, curd, paneer, butter or ghee
Sirf tel mein pakaiye, alag bartan ho to achha hai = please cook only in oil, separate utensils if possible

links.label: Incredible India — Andaman and Nicobar Islands
Go2Andaman ferry booking and island guides

cities.name: Port Blair
cities.notes: Gateway town for IXZ flights, Cellular Jail, Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Dweep/Ross Island, North Bay and the practical ferry buffer before or after island stays.

cities.name: Swaraj Dweep
cities.notes: Havelock/Swaraj Dweep is the main beach-and-dive base, anchored by Radhanagar Beach, Elephant Beach snorkelling and certified dive shops.

cities.name: Shaheed Dweep
cities.notes: Neil/Shaheed Dweep is smaller and quieter, with Bharatpur snorkelling, Laxmanpur sunset, Sitapur sunrise and the Natural Bridge coral-rock walk.

cities.name: Baratang
cities.notes: A long day-trip or one-night add-on from Port Blair for mangrove creeks, limestone caves and the mud volcano, feasible only with early convoy/boat logistics.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel work well around Port Blair and main settlements on Swaraj/Shaheed Dweep, while BSNL can be useful in remote pockets. Expect weak data on ferries, at Elephant Beach, Baratang mangrove creeks and some beach resorts; download ferry tickets, maps and hotel contacts offline.

Port Blair.name: Port Blair
Port Blair.note: Three days covers a light arrival, the Cellular Jail/Ross/North Bay heritage-water loop, and a final ferry-buffer or Wandoor/Chidiya Tapu day; keep at least one Port Blair night before your flight because ferry delays happen.

days.theme: Arrival, Corbyn's Cove and Cellular Jail show
days.note: IXZ arrivals, hotel check-in and humidity make this a light first day. Buy Cellular Jail light-and-sound tickets early because evening shows sell out in peak season.

activities.name: Arrive at IXZ and confirm ferry tickets
activities.tip: Keep printed/digital ferry tickets and photo ID handy; private ferry check-in usually closes well before departure.

activities.name: Corbyn's Cove Beach sunset walk
activities.tip: Good for a gentle first evening, not the cleanest swimming beach after rain.

activities.name: Cellular Jail light-and-sound show
activities.cost: ₹400 show ticket via 2026 booking pages; verify slot locally
activities.tip: Hindi and English evening slots vary by season; closed Mondays/national holidays for the museum.

days.theme: Cellular Jail, Ross Island and North Bay
days.note: Cluster the morning museum with the Aberdeen/jetty area, then take the harbour boat loop. Sea conditions can cancel North Bay or make glass-bottom rides rough.

activities.name: Cellular Jail National Memorial museum
activities.cost: ₹30 entry + camera fee; 2026 guide page still lists older government tariff
activities.tip: Go early or late afternoon; last museum tickets are commonly issued before closing.

activities.name: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Dweep/Ross Island ruins
activities.cost: Boat/entry ₹500–₹900/person band; verify current jetty rate
activities.tip: Carry water; deer and ruins are best explored on foot or with the island buggy if available.

activities.name: North Bay Island glass-bottom boat or snorkel
activities.tip: Pick one water activity here if you are not doing Havelock/Neil snorkelling.

days.theme: Wandoor, Chidiya Tapu or ferry-buffer day
days.note: Use this day at the end of the route before your flight, or as a weather buffer if ferries shift. Jolly Buoy/Red Skin islands operate under seasonal permits and often close in monsoon or rotation periods.

activities.name: Wandoor Beach and Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park gate area
activities.cost: Free; island permits/boats extra when operating
activities.tip: Do not assume Jolly Buoy is open; check the current island rotation before planning.

activities.name: Chidiya Tapu sunset and birding drive
activities.tip: Best in clear weather; return before it gets too dark on forest roads.

activities.name: Ferry or flight buffer admin
activities.tip: Reconfirm the next morning's sailing/flight and keep a buffer night in Port Blair before flying home.

days.note: Add only if you want a slower Port Blair finish or need insurance against ferry disruption in rough-sea months.

activities.name: Anthropological Museum
activities.tip: Use the visit for context, but do not seek tribal contact or photography.

meals: Paneer curry if dairy is OK

Swaraj Dweep.name: Swaraj Dweep
Swaraj Dweep.note: Two full days are the minimum for Radhanagar plus one serious water day; add nights if you want certification dives or a resort-focused beach break.

days.theme: Ferry to Havelock and Radhanagar Beach
days.note: The Port Blair to Swaraj Dweep ferry takes roughly 1.5–2.5 hours, but hotel transfers, reporting time and luggage make this a half-day move. Do not schedule a paid dive immediately after arrival.

activities.name: Port Blair to Swaraj Dweep private/government ferry
activities.cost: Private catamaran ₹1,500–₹2,500/person band; government ferry usually cheaper but harder to book
activities.tip: Makruzz's Sep 2025–May 2026 schedule lists multiple Port Blair–Havelock sailings; fares are dynamic, so verify at booking.

activities.name: Radhanagar Beach sunset
activities.tip: Swim only inside safe zones and obey lifeguards; currents and closures change after rain.

days.theme: Scuba, Elephant Beach snorkelling and beach recovery
days.note: Water activities should stand alone because briefings, boat transfers, tides and no-fly-after-diving rules matter. Avoid scuba within 18–24 hours of a flight and skip if medically unfit.

activities.name: Beginner boat scuba dive
activities.tip: Use a certified dive centre; Go2Andaman's 2026 guide lists Havelock boat dives from ₹6,500 and courses from ₹32,000.

activities.name: Elephant Beach snorkelling by boat
activities.tip: Boats run only in safe sea conditions; some seasons switch to limited beach access or cancel.

meals: Banana pancake or poha before dive
Dal tadka without ghee if vegan

days.theme: Optional certification or slow beach day
days.note: Add this only for Open Water certification, repeat dives, kayaking or a genuine beach-resort pause. Certification courses need multiple days and should not be squeezed into a transfer day.

activities.name: Second fun dive or Open Water course session
activities.cost: Fun dives from ₹6,600; Open Water courses from ₹32,000 on 2026 pages
activities.tip: Keep your final dive at least 18–24 hours before flying.

activities.name: Mangrove kayaking or night kayaking
activities.tip: Bioluminescence depends on moon phase and tide.

activities.name: Radhanagar repeat swim or resort downtime

meals: Veg pasta at a resort café

Shaheed Dweep.name: Shaheed Dweep
Shaheed Dweep.note: One night covers the ferry transfer plus Bharatpur, Laxmanpur and Natural Bridge if tides cooperate; add a second for a very slow beach stay.

days.theme: Ferry to Neil, Bharatpur snorkel and Laxmanpur sunset
days.note: The Havelock–Neil ferry is short, but fixed timings control the whole day. Visit Natural Bridge only around low tide and keep reef shoes or sturdy sandals for sharp coral rock.

activities.name: Swaraj Dweep to Shaheed Dweep ferry
activities.cost: Private ferry ₹1,200–₹2,000/person band; verify at booking
activities.tip: Makruzz's 2025–2026 schedule lists Havelock–Neil morning and afternoon connections on some rotations.

activities.name: Bharatpur Beach snorkelling or glass-bottom boat
activities.tip: Best in clear calm water; avoid stepping on coral.

activities.name: Natural Bridge low-tide walk
activities.tip: Go only at low tide; guides help spot marine life without damaging tide pools.

days.theme: Optional Sitapur sunrise and slow Neil day
days.note: Use this optional second day if you want a quiet island pace or if ferry timings force an extra night; Neil is small and not a checklist destination.

activities.name: Return ferry timing buffer
activities.tip: Do not miss the sailing; last-minute seats off Neil can be scarce in peak season.

Baratang.name: Baratang
Baratang.note: Baratang is logistics-heavy: start before dawn from Port Blair, follow the convoy/ATR rules, take the mangrove boat to limestone caves, and avoid any stop or photography in tribal reserve areas.

days.theme: Limestone caves and mud volcano from Port Blair
days.note: This is a long controlled-route day, not a casual beach outing. Leave around 3:30–4:00am, carry ID, snacks and water, and never photograph or interact with protected tribal communities along the route.

activities.name: Port Blair to Baratang ATR/convoy transfer
activities.tip: Convoy timings and road rules change; book through a responsible operator and expect a very early start.

activities.name: Mangrove creek boat to limestone caves
activities.cost: Often included in tour; boat/permit component varies
activities.tip: Wear non-slip sandals; cave access can close in heavy rain or high water.

activities.name: Baratang mud volcano
activities.tip: Interesting but modest; treat it as an add-on, not the sole reason for the long day.

activities.name: Return to Port Blair before late evening

days.note: Use only if you dislike very long same-day drives or if ferry/convoy timing makes the caves rushed. Accommodation and food are basic compared with Port Blair/Havelock.

activities.name: Slow mangrove creek repeat or local jetty walk
Rest and early return to Port Blair
Buffer for weather, road or boat disruption

connections.method: Government ferry or private catamaran such as Makruzz/Nautika/Green Ocean from Haddo/Phoenix Bay jetty
connections.cost: Private ferry ₹1,500–₹2,500/person band; government ferry cheaper if tickets available

connections.method: Private/government inter-island ferry with fixed morning or afternoon sailing
Return private/government ferry; keep one Port Blair night before flights
ATR road convoy plus vehicle ferry/creek boat, usually as a guided day tour

extras: Ferries dominate feasibility: private operators publish fixed seasonal schedules, government ferries are cheaper but harder to book, and rough seas or technical changes can alter sailings; reserve seats early and keep a Port Blair buffer before flights.
Only permitted Andaman areas should be planned for ordinary tourism. Nicobar Islands and tribal reserves are restricted; never attempt contact, photography or detours involving protected tribes.
Best weather is October to May. June to September brings heavy monsoon rain, rougher seas, cancelled boats and limited water sports, though hotels may be cheaper.

Scuba and snorkelling are safest with certified operators in calm seas; do not dive before flying, and avoid scuba if pregnant or with uncontrolled asthma, heart, blood-pressure or diabetes concerns unless cleared by a doctor.
Carry cash on smaller islands, reef-safe sun protection, dry bags for ferry luggage and backup vegetarian/vegan snacks for early transfers.
